Meaning: To increase the number of words you know
Example Sentence from Video: I want to help you build your vocabulary by learning collocations.
Mnemonic: BUILD like a BUILDING — you add bricks (words) one by one to make it taller.

Meaning: To improve and expand your word knowledge
Example Sentence from Video: You should develop your vocabulary to sound more natural.
Mnemonic: Think of DEVELOPING a photo — hidden details become clearer over time, just like new words reveal meaning.

Meaning: To make your vocabulary wider and broader
Example Sentence from Video: Learning collocations will expand your vocabulary.
Mnemonic: EXPAND = spread outward. Imagine your vocabulary stretching like an expanding universe.

Meaning: A very large quantity of something
Example Sentence from Video: Building your vocabulary takes a considerable amount of time.
Mnemonic: CONSIDER + ABLE = something worth considering because it's so big.

Meaning: A very significant change or impact
Example Sentence from Video: Learning collocations will make a considerable difference.
Mnemonic: Considerable = CONSIDERABLY big enough to turn heads.

Meaning: A lot of advancement or improvement
Example Sentence from Video: If you follow my lessons, you will make considerable progress.
Mnemonic: Imagine a CONSIDERABLE distance covered on a progress bar — it's nearly full.

Meaning: A great power to affect people or events
Example Sentence from Video: Use your considerable influence to convince your colleagues.
Mnemonic: CONSIDERable = so big, you must CONSIDER its weight.

Meaning: To share your opinion or evaluation of something
Example Sentence from Video: You may give feedback or provide feedback to help improve something.
Mnemonic: GIVE = hand over your thoughts like a gift wrapped in opinion.

Meaning: To offer information or opinions about performance
Example Sentence from Video: A teacher will provide feedback on your writing.
Mnemonic: PROVIDE = supply. You supply your reaction like fuel.

Meaning: To volunteer your comments or opinions
Example Sentence from Video: She offered feedback on my presentation to help me improve.
Mnemonic: OFFER = put on the table. Like laying your thoughts out for someone to pick up.

Meaning: To get comments or evaluations from someone else
Example Sentence from Video: I received feedback that my lesson was very helpful.
Mnemonic: RE-CEIVE = take IN. You receive feedback like receiving a letter.

Meaning: Comments that praise or encourage
Example Sentence from Video: Overwhelmingly, people want to receive positive feedback.
Mnemonic: POSITIVE = plus sign (+). Add good energy to someone's effort.

Meaning: Comments that criticize or point out flaws
Example Sentence from Video: Negative feedback is also important for growth.
Mnemonic: NEGATIVE = minus sign (−). It subtracts comfort but can add improvement.

Meaning: Extremely anxious or concerned about something
Example Sentence from Video: I am deeply worried about aging.
Mnemonic: DEEPLY = dig DOWN deep into worry. It's not surface-level — it's underground-level stress.

Meaning: Very seriously troubled or anxious
Example Sentence from Video: The US is deeply concerned about an escalating conflict.
Mnemonic: DEEPLY + CONCERNED: Imagine being so concerned you dig a hole worrying about it.

Meaning: Having very serious weaknesses or imperfections
Example Sentence from Video: He's deeply flawed, and somehow we raised a child.
Mnemonic: A DEEPLY cracked vase — the flaw goes all the way through.

Meaning: Sincerely and profoundly apologetic
Example Sentence from Video: He is deeply sorry for what he did.
Mnemonic: DEEPLY SORRY = sorry from the soul, not just the surface.

Meaning: Strongly affected emotionally by something
Example Sentence from Video: I told him I was deeply moved by that statement.
Mnemonic: MOVED = emotion moved you so deeply you nearly fell over.

Meaning: Very intensely in love with someone
Example Sentence from Video: We were so deeply in love with each other.
Mnemonic: DEEPLY IN LOVE = love went underground — all the way to your core.

Meaning: A topic that causes strong disagreement
Example Sentence from Video: They were debating a controversial issue in parliament.
Mnemonic: CONTRO-VERSY = counter-version. Two opposing versions of the truth fighting it out.

Meaning: A choice that many people disagree with
Example Sentence from Video: He made a controversial decision that divided the team.
Mnemonic: Imagine making a decision and the whole room SPLITS in two.

Meaning: A person who causes disagreement or strong reaction
Example Sentence from Video: She is a highly controversial figure in politics.
Mnemonic: A FIGURE = shape. A controversial figure is one that casts a jagged shadow.

Meaning: Causing very strong disagreement or debate
Example Sentence from Video: This is a highly controversial topic in today's world.
Mnemonic: HIGHLY = stacked high. The controversy piles up like a skyscraper.

Meaning: The generally accepted belief or opinion
Example Sentence from Video: Conventional wisdom suggests we should resume hostilities.
Mnemonic: CONVENTIONAL = ordinary, common. Wisdom that has become the default setting of society.

Meaning: The last molar teeth that grow in adulthood
Example Sentence from Video: I'm getting my wisdom teeth pulled.
Mnemonic: Called WISDOM teeth because they arrive when you're supposedly wiser (as an adult). Ironic if they hurt.

Meaning: To arrange for a meeting to take place
Example Sentence from Video: The manager decided to call an emergency meeting.
Mnemonic: CALL = summon. You're calling everyone in, like calling your dog's name.

Meaning: To formally demand that workers stop working
Example Sentence from Video: Union leaders called for a strike after talks broke down.
Mnemonic: CALL FOR = demand loudly. Imagine workers chanting as their leader CALLS FOR action.

Meaning: To officially announce that an election will take place
Example Sentence from Video: The prime minister called for an election after losing support.
Mnemonic: CALLING voters to the booth — the leader picks up the phone and calls for democracy.

Meaning: To demand that something stops immediately
Example Sentence from Video: Activists called for a halt to the logging of trees.
Mnemonic: HALT = freeze! And someone's calling that command loudly.

Meaning: A boring or uninteresting piece of work
Example Sentence from Video: Jury selection is tedious work.
Mnemonic: TED-IOUS = imagine a very boring TED Talk you can't escape. That's tedious.

Meaning: A dull and repetitive activity
Example Sentence from Video: It was the only way to get through yet another tedious and useless task.
Mnemonic: TEDIOUS TASK = Two T's that together triple the boredom.

Meaning: To be overflowing with great enthusiasm
Example Sentence from Video: I am bubbling over with excitement to teach more collocations.
Mnemonic: Imagine boiling water — the BUBBLES are excitement OVERFLOWING the pot.

Meaning: To show overflowing eagerness and energy
Example Sentence from Video: She was bubbling over with enthusiasm for the new project.
Mnemonic: BUBBLE = unstoppable upward energy. Add enthusiasm and it's a party.

Meaning: A very strong and intense wish for something
Example Sentence from Video: Firefighters have passion; arsonists have a burning desire.
Mnemonic: BURNING = fire in your chest. The desire is so hot it could ignite something.

Meaning: An extremely strong motivation to succeed
Example Sentence from Video: Her burning ambition to become CEO drove her forward.
Mnemonic: AMBITION on FIRE — imagine your goal tied to a lit fuse.

Meaning: An urgent problem that must be addressed quickly
Example Sentence from Video: There are many burning issues facing the country today.
Mnemonic: BURNING = so hot it demands attention NOW before it turns into an inferno.

Meaning: An important question that needs an urgent answer
Example Sentence from Video: You look like a boy with a burning question.
Mnemonic: The question is so HOT it's practically smoking — it can't wait.

Meaning: To fascinate or interest someone deeply
Example Sentence from Video: This lesson has captured your imagination.
Mnemonic: CAPTURE = trap. The idea traps your imagination like a butterfly in a jar.

Meaning: To attract and hold someone's focus
Example Sentence from Video: The speaker captured the audience's attention immediately.
Mnemonic: CAPTURE ATTENTION = snatch it. Like a magician pulling focus.

Meaning: To spark and maintain someone's curiosity
Example Sentence from Video: I hope this lesson has captured your interest.
Mnemonic: CAPTURE INTEREST = hook it. Like a fishing line reeling in your curiosity.

Meaning: The real or hidden reason behind something
Example Sentence from Video: The doctor examined the underlying cause of his illness.
Mnemonic: UNDER + LYING = hiding underneath. The real cause is beneath the surface, lying low.

Meaning: A pre-existing health issue that affects treatment
Example Sentence from Video: The mortality rate depends upon underlying medical conditions.
Mnemonic: Think of a hidden crack UNDER the foundation of a building.

Meaning: The fundamental rule or belief behind something
Example Sentence from Video: We must understand the underlying principle of this policy.
Mnemonic: It's the BEDROCK — the principle lying under everything else.

Meaning: A hidden or non-obvious reason driving behavior
Example Sentence from Video: Wanting to date in English might be an underlying motivation for learning.
Mnemonic: UNDER = beneath awareness. The motivation lurks below the surface.

Meaning: A system of laws that governs a situation
Example Sentence from Video: We need a legal framework to regulate the industry.
Mnemonic: FRAME + WORK: Laws are the frame — the skeleton — that holds society's structure together.

Meaning: Rules and structures that control an industry
Example Sentence from Video: The regulatory framework ensures businesses follow the rules.
Mnemonic: REGULATE = control. The framework is the rulebook's bookshelf.

Meaning: A set of ideas used to explain or analyze something
Example Sentence from Video: Researchers used a theoretical framework to interpret the data.
Mnemonic: THEORY as a FRAME: ideas form the scaffolding around knowledge.

Meaning: The total money spent by the government
Example Sentence from Video: We need to examine government expenditure carefully.
Mnemonic: EXPEND = spend. Government EXPENDITURE = the national credit card bill.

Meaning: The total amount of money spent in one year
Example Sentence from Video: The company reviewed its annual expenditure last quarter.
Mnemonic: ANNUAL = yearly. Every year you total up what you EXPENDED.

Meaning: To reduce the amount of money being spent
Example Sentence from Video: We need to cut expenditures by 32%.
Mnemonic: CUT = scissors through spending. Snip snip — the budget shrinks.

Meaning: The original or exact meaning of a word or phrase
Example Sentence from Video: Look up the literal meaning of that term.
Mnemonic: LITERAL = the LETTER of what it says. No metaphor, no imagination — face value.

Meaning: An understanding based on the exact words used
Example Sentence from Video: The judge applied a literal interpretation of the law.
Mnemonic: LITERAL interpretation = reading the map exactly as printed, no creative routes.

Meaning: Resources that have a limited supply and will run out
Example Sentence from Video: We must use our finite resources wisely.
Mnemonic: FI-NITE = has a FINISH. Resources that will eventually end.

Meaning: A number that has a definite limit
Example Sentence from Video: There are only a finite number of opportunities.
Mnemonic: FINITE = FIN (end in French). There's a definite ending point.

Meaning: To express very strong disapproval of something
Example Sentence from Video: World leaders strongly condemned the attack.
Mnemonic: STRONGLY = with force. CONDEMN = sentence to blame. Powerful moral judgment.

Meaning: To have a very firm opposing opinion
Example Sentence from Video: I strongly disagree with that assessment.
Mnemonic: STRONGLY DISAGREE = dig your heels in. Not budging an inch.

Meaning: To firmly recommend a course of action
Example Sentence from Video: I strongly suggest that you rewatch this lesson.
Mnemonic: STRONGLY SUGGEST = politely firm. Still a suggestion but said through gritted teeth.

Meaning: To very firmly advise someone to do something
Example Sentence from Video: I strongly recommend visiting a doctor immediately.
Mnemonic: STRONGLY RECOMMEND = even firmer. Not just advice — near-instruction.

Meaning: To be very firmly in favor of something
Example Sentence from Video: The committee strongly supports this proposal.
Mnemonic: STRONGLY SUPPORT = stand behind something with both hands pushing.

Meaning: Completely conscious and informed about something
Example Sentence from Video: I am fully aware that you won't memorize all of these.
Mnemonic: FULLY = 100%. AWARE = eyes wide open. Complete knowledge.

Meaning: Completely alert and not sleepy
Example Sentence from Video: I hope you are fully awake right now.
Mnemonic: FULLY AWAKE = no trace of sleep. Coffee achieved maximum effect.

Meaning: Working completely and at full capacity
Example Sentence from Video: The machine is now fully operational.
Mnemonic: FULLY OPERATIONAL = all systems go. Like a spaceship ready for launch.

Meaning: Completely astonished or surprised
Example Sentence from Video: I was absolutely amazed when I saw the movie.
Mnemonic: ABSOLUTELY = without any doubt. AMAZED = jaw dropped. No qualifier needed.

Meaning: Completely shocked and disgusted
Example Sentence from Video: She was absolutely appalled by the rude comment.
Mnemonic: APPALLED sounds like A-PALLED — like someone turned pale with shock.

Meaning: Completely and totally isolated
Example Sentence from Video: After the breakup, he felt utterly alone.
Mnemonic: UTTERLY = completely, with no exceptions. ALONE with an exclamation point.

Meaning: Completely certain about something
Example Sentence from Video: I am utterly convinced you will learn these collocations.
Mnemonic: UTTERLY CONVINCED = belief cemented. No cracks, no doubts.

Meaning: Completely overwhelmed with grief or sadness
Example Sentence from Video: We were absolutely devastated when we heard the news.
Mnemonic: DEVASTATED = de-VAST-ated. Like a vast storm wiped everything away.

Meaning: Extremely angry
Example Sentence from Video: She was absolutely furious when she found out.
Mnemonic: FURIOUS = fuming fire. ABSOLUTELY adds fuel to the flame.

Meaning: Extremely unhappy or uncomfortable
Example Sentence from Video: I got soaking wet and felt absolutely miserable.
Mnemonic: MISERABLE = sounds like MIS-ery +ERABLE = capable of feeling maximum misery.

Meaning: Completely unreasonable or absurd
Example Sentence from Video: It was absolutely ridiculous what happened.
Mnemonic: RIDICULOUS = RIDICULE + OUS. So silly it laughs at itself.

Meaning: Very seriously troubled or worried
Example Sentence from Video: People are deeply concerned about the situation.
Mnemonic: DEEPLY CONCERNED = worry that goes to your bones.

Meaning: To feel very strong remorse about something
Example Sentence from Video: I deeply regret saying that to her.
Mnemonic: DEEPLY REGRET = the regret sinks into your chest and stays there.

Meaning: Very strongly split in opinion or belief
Example Sentence from Video: People are so deeply divided on this issue.
Mnemonic: DEEPLY DIVIDED = a canyon between two sides, not just a crack.

Meaning: Feeling very strong guilt or embarrassment
Example Sentence from Video: She was deeply ashamed of her behavior.
Mnemonic: DEEPLY ASHAMED = shame that sinks below the surface. Blush that goes inward.

Meaning: Very improbable or almost impossible
Example Sentence from Video: It is highly unlikely that this will happen.
Mnemonic: HIGHLY UNLIKELY = probability meter near zero. The bar is set impossibly high against it.

Meaning: To strongly advise as the very best option
Example Sentence from Video: I highly recommend trying this restaurant.
Mnemonic: HIGHLY = top rating. RECOMMEND = best review. Five stars, no hesitation.

Meaning: Very strange or uncommon
Example Sentence from Video: Her behavior was highly unusual for the circumstances.
Mnemonic: HIGHLY UNUSUAL = so rare it floats above the normal range.

Meaning: Causing very intense disagreement
Example Sentence from Video: The policy was highly controversial and sparked debate.
Mnemonic: HIGHLY CONTROVERSIAL = controversy stacked to the ceiling.

Meaning: Very complex and advanced
Example Sentence from Video: The technology is highly sophisticated.
Mnemonic: SOPHIS-TICATED = wise + aged. HIGHLY adds extra layers of complexity.

Meaning: Having a very high level of formal education
Example Sentence from Video: She is a highly educated individual with a PhD.
Mnemonic: HIGHLY EDUCATED = climbed all the way up the academic ladder.

Meaning: Very smart and mentally sharp
Example Sentence from Video: You are all highly intelligent learners.
Mnemonic: HIGHLY = topped off. Intelligence meter: full.

Meaning: Earning a very large salary
Example Sentence from Video: She is highly paid for her expertise.
Mnemonic: HIGH PAY = salary sitting in the penthouse of the pay scale.

Meaning: Greatly overstated and far from the truth
Example Sentence from Video: The story was grossly exaggerated by the media.
Mnemonic: GROSS = disgusting exaggeration — so far from truth it's offensive.

Meaning: Extremely careless or irresponsible
Example Sentence from Video: The doctor was found grossly negligent in the case.
Mnemonic: GROSS NEGLIGENCE = negligence so big it makes you say 'gross!'.

Meaning: Very badly wrong or incorrect
Example Sentence from Video: That report was grossly inaccurate.
Mnemonic: GROSS INACCURACY = error so large it's uncomfortable to witness.

Meaning: Extremely unjust or unreasonable
Example Sentence from Video: The punishment was grossly unfair.
Mnemonic: GROSSLY UNFAIR = fairness scale tipped so far it broke.

Meaning: Very seriously or severely treated badly
Example Sentence from Video: The workers were grossly mistreated by management.
Mnemonic: MISTREATED beyond measure — GROSSLY adds the extra layer of severity.

Meaning: Judged to be far less than the actual amount or ability
Example Sentence from Video: We grossly underestimated the results.
Mnemonic: Think: you guessed a GROSS number too low. Way off the mark.

Meaning: Slightly or moderately bothered or annoyed
Example Sentence from Video: She was only mildly upset by the comment.
Mnemonic: MILDLY = like mild salsa. Just a little heat, not buffalo sauce.

Meaning: Slightly worried but not seriously alarmed
Example Sentence from Video: He was mildly concerned about the weather forecast.
Mnemonic: MILD concern = eyebrow slightly raised, not both.

Meaning: Slightly taken aback or unexpectedly informed
Example Sentence from Video: I was mildly surprised to see her there.
Mnemonic: MILDLY SURPRISED = small eyes wide. Not a gasp, just a raised brow.

Meaning: Accessible in many places or to many people
Example Sentence from Video: This product is widely available at most stores.
Mnemonic: WIDELY = spread across a wide area. Available EVERYWHERE.

Meaning: Known by a lot of people in general
Example Sentence from Video: It is widely known that exercise is good for health.
Mnemonic: WIDELY KNOWN = knowledge broadcast across a wide signal.

Meaning: Believed or agreed upon by most people
Example Sentence from Video: Evolution is widely accepted in the scientific community.
Mnemonic: WIDELY ACCEPTED = hands raised everywhere in agreement.

Meaning: Completely usual and not strange at all
Example Sentence from Video: It is perfectly normal to make mistakes.
Mnemonic: PERFECTLY NORMAL = textbook standard. Nothing to see here.

Meaning: Completely fine and appropriate
Example Sentence from Video: His behavior was perfectly acceptable in that context.
Mnemonic: PERFECTLY ACCEPTABLE = passes all the tests. Zero complaints.

Meaning: Completely happy and ready to do something
Example Sentence from Video: I am perfectly willing to help you with that.
Mnemonic: PERFECTLY WILLING = green light from all systems. Ready to go.

Meaning: A difficult and unpleasant day
Example Sentence from Video: We've had a bit of a rough day after the airline destroyed our stroller.
Mnemonic: ROUGH = uneven surface. A rough day has lots of bumps and scratches.

Meaning: An early incomplete version of written work
Example Sentence from Video: I've only done a rough draft so far.
Mnemonic: ROUGH = unfinished. DRAFT = first attempt. Like sketching before painting.

Meaning: An approximate guess or calculation
Example Sentence from Video: Can you give me a rough estimate of the cost?
Mnemonic: ROUGH = unpolished. The estimate has jagged edges — it's not exact.

Meaning: A dangerous or unsafe area
Example Sentence from Video: I grew up in a rough neighborhood.
Mnemonic: ROUGH = uneven, dangerous. The neighborhood has sharp edges.

Meaning: A small amount of something
Example Sentence from Video: We'll give it a touch of paint and it will be finished.
Mnemonic: TOUCH = a finger's amount. Just a tiny contact of something.

Meaning: The last small detail that completes something
Example Sentence from Video: She added the finishing touches to the painting.
Mnemonic: FINISHING TOUCH = the final stroke of the brush. The moment something becomes done.

Meaning: A detail that improves or enhances something
Example Sentence from Video: Adding candles was a nice touch at the dinner.
Mnemonic: NICE TOUCH = a gentle, welcome addition. Like a bow on a gift.

Meaning: A gentle and delicate way of handling something
Example Sentence from Video: She has a soft touch when dealing with nervous patients.
Mnemonic: SOFT TOUCH = velvet gloves. Delicate and comforting contact.

Meaning: To do something better than anyone has done before
Example Sentence from Video: Someday I hope to break a world record.
Mnemonic: BREAK the old record like breaking a chain — free from the previous limit.

Meaning: Good luck (informal theatrical expression)
Example Sentence from Video: Before the show, I told her to break a leg.
Mnemonic: BREAK A LEG = twisted theater logic. Breaking = smashing success. Legs = stage presence.

Meaning: To fail to do what you said you would do
Example Sentence from Video: I hope you don't break your promise to me.
Mnemonic: BREAK = shatter. A broken promise is like broken glass — hard to fix.

Meaning: To make a social situation more relaxed and comfortable
Example Sentence from Video: I do activities on the first day to break the ice.
Mnemonic: ICE = cold tension. Breaking it warms things up. Social thaw.

Meaning: To do something that is illegal
Example Sentence from Video: I hope that you never break the law.
Mnemonic: BREAK THE LAW = shatter the rule that holds society together. Dangerous.

Meaning: To do something helpful for another person
Example Sentence from Video: I need you to do me a huge favor.
Mnemonic: FAVOR = a gift of effort. You DO it for them — no cost to them.

Meaning: To do cleaning and other chores around the home
Example Sentence from Video: Nobody wants to do housework but it needs to be done.
Mnemonic: HOUSEWORK = the job your HOUSE demands of you.

Meaning: To wash clothes
Example Sentence from Video: She's going to do her hair and nails and then do the laundry.
Mnemonic: DO THE LAUNDRY = fulfilling the contract your dirty clothes wrote.

Meaning: To wash plates
Example Sentence from Video: cups
Mnemonic: and utensils after eating
column7: Can you do the dishes tonight?
column8: DO THE DISHES = the dishes call you by name after every meal.

Meaning: To try as hard as you possibly can
Example Sentence from Video: I just want you to do your best.
Mnemonic: DO YOUR BEST = give 100%. No more, no less — just everything you've got.

Meaning: To cause damage or hurt to someone or something
Example Sentence from Video: You don't want to do harm to others.
Mnemonic: DO HARM = an action that damages. Doing when you shouldn't.

Meaning: To use time on a particular activity
Example Sentence from Video: I spend too much time editing these videos.
Mnemonic: SPEND TIME = time is currency. Once spent, it's gone.

Meaning: To use time on useless or unimportant activities
Example Sentence from Video: You're certainly not wasting your time learning collocations.
Mnemonic: WASTE TIME = throw your minutes in the bin.

Meaning: To do something unimportant while waiting
Example Sentence from Video: I walked around the mall to kill time.
Mnemonic: KILL TIME = commit temporal murder. Time dies so you can survive waiting.

Meaning: To do something to occupy yourself while waiting
Example Sentence from Video: I like to read books to pass the time.
Mnemonic: PASS THE TIME = hand time a ticket to move along. Keep it moving.

Meaning: Arriving or happening at exactly the correct moment
Example Sentence from Video: I try to be right on time, but it doesn't always happen.
Mnemonic: RIGHT ON TIME = bullseye timing. Hit the clock dead center.

Meaning: To have no fuel remaining in the vehicle
Example Sentence from Video: The car is about to run out of gas.
Mnemonic: RUN OUT = chase until empty. The tank ran and emptied itself.

Meaning: To no longer be able to tolerate a situation calmly
Example Sentence from Video: Be careful — I'm running out of patience.
Mnemonic: PATIENCE meter emptying like an hourglass. Sand nearly gone.

Meaning: To have no more time available
Example Sentence from Video: We ran out of time before finishing the project.
Mnemonic: TIME RAN AWAY. You chased it but it escaped.

Meaning: To prepare coffee for drinking
Example Sentence from Video: In the morning, I usually make some coffee.
Mnemonic: MAKE = create. Coffee doesn't exist until you MAKE it happen.

Meaning: To decide on a course of action
Example Sentence from Video: We need to make a plan before starting the project.
Mnemonic: MAKE A PLAN = construct a roadmap. Blueprint before building.

Meaning: To choose between options and commit to one
Example Sentence from Video: You need to make a decision soon.
Mnemonic: MAKE A DECISION = forge a choice. Like casting it in metal — firm.

Meaning: To do something incorrectly or wrongly
Example Sentence from Video: It's perfectly normal to make mistakes.
Mnemonic: MAKE A MISTAKE = accidentally create an error. It was made, not chosen.

Meaning: To commit to doing something for someone
Example Sentence from Video: You can make a promise and then break it.
Mnemonic: MAKE A PROMISE = build a verbal contract. Handle with care.

Meaning: To earn income from work or business
Example Sentence from Video: Someday I hope to make a lot of money.
Mnemonic: MAKE MONEY = your effort produces currency. Like minting your own coins.

Meaning: To earn enough money to support yourself
Example Sentence from Video: That's how people make a living.
Mnemonic: MAKE A LIVING = craft your survival. Every job builds this.

Meaning: To give a false or weak reason for something
Example Sentence from Video: If you break a promise, you can always make an excuse.
Mnemonic: MAKE AN EXCUSE = manufacture a reason. Factory output: not entirely true.

Meaning: To spend money carelessly on unimportant things
Example Sentence from Video: You shouldn't be wasting money on things you don't need.
Mnemonic: WASTE MONEY = pour it down the drain. Gone with nothing to show.

Meaning: To put money into something expecting a return
Example Sentence from Video: You can invest money in stocks or real estate.
Mnemonic: INVEST = plant money like a seed. Expect growth later.

Meaning: To give money to a charity or good cause
Example Sentence from Video: If you're feeling charitable, you can donate money.
Mnemonic: DONATE = give with an open palm. No strings, no return expected.

Meaning: To keep money rather than spending it
Example Sentence from Video: It's always a good idea to save money.
Mnemonic: SAVE MONEY = rescue it from spending. Put it in protective custody.

Meaning: To reserve money for a specific purpose
Example Sentence from Video: We try to set aside some money for a rainy day.
Mnemonic: SET ASIDE = move it out of reach of temptation. Reserved.
